description: Structural conventions for assembling config JSON from registry data. Read by builder.py and validator.py. The 4 domain registries (models, systems, states, algorithms) define WHAT is available; this file defines HOW to assemble it.

backend_categories.description: Maps each algorithm to its backend category. The builder uses this instead of hardcoding algorithm names.

model_assembly.description: How to assemble the model block. Declares which system fields get copied into model.params, and what format custom models use per backend.

prebuilt.description: For prebuilt models, model.params always includes these system fields (for hash consistency). The builder copies them from the completed system block.

custom.description: Custom models use different format keys and extra params depending on backend category and system type. format_key is the key under model.params that holds the user's terms/channels. spinboson_sub_keys maps the 3 term categories to their backend-specific sub-key names.

state_assembly.description: How to assemble state blocks. Field names and nesting differ by backend category and system type.

custom.description: Custom states: site_field is the key name for the per-site array. location is where these fields go (root = directly on state object, params = nested inside state.params).

random.description: Random states include bond_dim only for TN algorithms. ED random states ignore bond_dim.

prebuilt.description: Prebuilt states use the same format for both backends. Polarized uses a different eigenstate field name for spinboson systems.

algorithm_assembly.description: How to build algorithm blocks. The builder reads config_structure from algorithms.json and uses this to map flat user params into the nested structure.

flat_param_mapping.description: Maps flat user-provided param names to their nested location in the algorithm config_structure. The builder uses this to accept {chi_max: 128} and place it at algorithm.options.chi_max. Dot notation indicates nesting.

auto_derived_fields.description: Fields computed from system params, not user-provided. The builder evaluates the formula and inserts the result at the target path.

solver_defaults.description: Fixed solver type strings per algorithm. These are always injected and never user-configurable.

analysis_assembly.description: How to map algorithm type to the correct selection key in analysis configs. Each algorithm produces output indexed by a different dimension.

dtype_resolution.description: How to auto-resolve dtype when the user hasn't set it explicitly. For prebuilt models: evaluate dtype_logic rules from models.json. For custom models: scan all operator strings against the spin_operators registry.

custom_model_scan.description: For custom models, extract all spin operator strings from the terms/channels. If any operator has real=false in systems.json spin_operators.operators, dtype must be ComplexF64. Otherwise Float64 is safe.
